message:
test(core): comprehensive retry module test suite (58 tests, was 9)

Add 49 new tests covering: RetryPolicy::new() clamping (backoff_factor,
jitter_percent), default trait values, delay_for_attempt edge cases
(zero initial, high attempt cap, constant backoff), all preset policy
fields, is_retryable for every Error variant (Io, Wezterm×8, Storage×8,
Pattern, Workflow, Config, Policy, Json, Runtime, SetupError, Cancelled,
Panicked), with_smart_retry (stops on non-retryable, retries retryable,
exhausts attempts), RetryOutcome tracking, and circuit breaker success path.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"wa-oegrb.4.2","title":"Implement incremental Tantivy ingestion from append-log checkpoints","description":"## Context
Recorder writes are append-only; indexer must consume offsets incrementally with deterministic resume behavior and bounded lag.

## Scope
Implement incremental index ingestion from recorder checkpoints, including idempotent retries and commit cadence controls.

## Deliverables
- Offset-driven incremental indexer pipeline
- Checkpoint coordination with storage replay cursors
- Commit/flush policy for freshness vs throughput tradeoffs
- Integration tests for restart/resume without data loss

## Why this matters
Incremental indexing is the bridge between high-throughput logging and usable near-real-time search.

## Acceptance
- Indexer resumes deterministically after restarts
- No silent drops/duplication under normal retry paths
- Lag is measurable and bounded under target load","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WildBeaver","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.499041Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T16:58:55.057711-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-12T16:58:55.057711-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented incremental Tantivy ingestion pipeline: AppendLogReader (binary log scanning, seek-to-ordinal, torn-tail safe), IndexDocumentFields (all 24 schema fields), map_event_to_document for all 4 event types with redaction handling, IndexWriter trait for pluggable backends, IncrementalIndexer with checkpoint-coordinated batching/resume/dedup, IndexerLagMonitor. 40 tests covering cold start, resume from checkpoint, multi-pane, mixed types, schema mismatch skip, commit failure propagation, lag monitoring.","labels":["epic","flight-recorder","implementation","tantivy","track-indexing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.4.2","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.499041Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.4.2","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.652992Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.4.2","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.3.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.911617Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"wa-oegrb.4.3","title":"Tune Tantivy commit/merge policy for sustained recorder ingest and query latency","description":"## Context
Tantivy segment management strongly affects write amplification and query latency. We need explicit merge and commit strategies aligned with recorder workload.

## Scope
Tune and codify segment lifecycle policy (commit frequency, merge heuristics, memory budgets) for terminal transcript data.

## Deliverables
- Merge policy configuration and rationale
- Benchmarked commit/merge profiles for common load regimes
- Operational toggles for safe runtime tuning
- Tests/benchmarks guarding against pathological segment explosion

## Why this matters
Poor segment strategy can make a correct index unusably slow or expensive.

## Acceptance
- Query latency and ingest throughput remain within target envelopes
- Segment growth remains bounded/predictable
- Policy is tunable without code surgery","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.530604Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T16:37:07.222087-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-12T16:14:43.246326-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented in tantivy_policy.rs: LoadRegime, CommitPolicy, MergePolicyConfig, MergePolicyEvaluator, CommitTracker, AdaptivePolicyController. 45 tests pass.","labels":["epic","flight-recorder","performance","track-indexing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.4.3","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.530604Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.4.3","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.681399Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"wa-oegrb.4.4","title":"Implement deterministic reindex/backfill tooling from recorder source-of-truth log","description":"## Context
We need deterministic rebuild/backfill tooling for schema evolution, corruption recovery, and historical imports.

## Scope
Create tooling to rebuild lexical index from recorder log ranges, with progress reporting and safety checks.

## Deliverables
- Full reindex command path with resumable checkpoints
- Partial/range backfill support by time/offset
- Integrity checks comparing index docs to source offsets
- Operator runbook for rebuild scenarios

## Why this matters
Without robust rebuild tooling, index incidents become high-risk and long-duration.

## Acceptance
- Rebuild can be run safely on large datasets
- Progress and completion criteria are explicit
- Integrity validation detects missing/corrupt ranges","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.562741Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.711435Z","labels":["epic","flight-recorder","operations","track-indexing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.4.4","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.562741Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.4.4","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.711388Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
-{"id":"wa-oegrb.4.5","title":"Build lexical query service over Tantivy with stable filters/ranking/snippets","description":"## Context
Hybrid search still depends on high-quality lexical retrieval as both baseline and fallback. Query behavior must be explicit, testable, and stable.

## Scope
Implement lexical query service (filters, ranking, snippets/highlights, pagination) over the Tantivy index.

## Deliverables
- Query API for lexical search with deterministic sorting/pagination
- Filter support (time, pane, session, agent, direction, tags)
- Highlight/snippet strategy suitable for terminal text
- Relevance regression tests and fixture corpus

## Why this matters
Lexical search is the reliability anchor; semantic rankers should enhance, not replace, this foundation.

## Acceptance
- Lexical results are accurate and predictable
- Filters and pagination semantics are stable
- Fixture-based tests protect behavior from regressions","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.592975Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.768296Z","labels":["epic","flight-recorder","query","track-indexing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.4.5","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.592975Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.4.5","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.740105Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.4.5","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.768214Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
+{"id":"wa-oegrb.4.5","title":"Build lexical query service over Tantivy with stable filters/ranking/snippets","description":"## Context
Hybrid search still depends on high-quality lexical retrieval as both baseline and fallback. Query behavior must be explicit, testable, and stable.

## Scope
Implement lexical query service (filters, ranking, snippets/highlights, pagination) over the Tantivy index.

## Deliverables
- Query API for lexical search with deterministic sorting/pagination
- Filter support (time, pane, session, agent, direction, tags)
- Highlight/snippet strategy suitable for terminal text
- Relevance regression tests and fixture corpus

## Why this matters
Lexical search is the reliability anchor; semantic rankers should enhance, not replace, this foundation.

## Acceptance
- Lexical results are accurate and predictable
- Filters and pagination semantics are stable
- Fixture-based tests protect behavior from regressions","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WildBeaver","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.592975Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T16:59:44.611186-05:00","labels":["epic","flight-recorder","query","track-indexing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.4.5","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.592975Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.4.5","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.740105Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.4.5","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.768214Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"wa-oegrb.4.6","title":"Create lexical quality harness with golden queries, relevance assertions, and latency budgets","description":"## Context
Search correctness can silently degrade with tokenizer, schema, or ranking changes. We need explicit evaluation harnesses tied to expected outcomes.

## Scope
Establish lexical search quality harness (golden query set + relevance assertions + latency checks).

## Deliverables
- Golden query dataset derived from realistic terminal workflows
- Relevance assertions (must-hit docs, ordering tolerances)
- Latency budget checks per query class
- CI wiring for regression detection

## Why this matters
A formal harness converts subjective “search feels off” complaints into actionable, reproducible failures.

## Acceptance
- Harness runs in CI/local and fails on quality regressions
- Query corpus covers critical forensic/operator scenarios
- Metrics are baseline-tracked for future improvements","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.623998Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.796985Z","labels":["epic","flight-recorder","testing","track-indexing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.4.6","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.623998Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.4.6","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.4.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:20.796937Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"wa-oegrb.5","title":"Semantic track: embeddings + vector retrieval + hybrid rank fusion","description":"## Track objective
Implement semantic retrieval and hybrid lexical+semantic ranking over recorder data, reusing validated patterns from cass/xf where appropriate.

## Why this track exists
Pure lexical search misses semantically similar incidents and prior solutions. Hybrid ranking materially improves operator and agent recall in incident/debug workflows.

## Deliverables
- Embedding/chunking strategy for recorder corpus
- Vector retrieval subsystem and ANN strategy
- Rank-fusion strategy combining lexical and semantic evidence
- Offline relevance evaluation corpus and metrics

## Acceptance criteria
- Hybrid ranking behavior is deterministic enough to test and tune.
- Semantic subsystem can degrade gracefully without breaking lexical search.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:02:58.478129Z","created_by":"WildSpring","updated_at":"2026-02-12T06:09:16.792358Z","labels":["cass","hybrid","search","semantic","xf"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.5","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:02:58.478129Z","created_by":"WildSpring","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.5","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:09:16.792305Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}]}
 {"id":"wa-oegrb.5.1","title":"Define embedding provider interface and model-governance strategy","description":"## Context
Semantic retrieval needs a model/provider strategy that balances quality, latency, cost, privacy, and offline operability. This must be explicit before implementation.

## Scope
Define embedding provider interface, model selection rubric, and deployment modes (local/remote) for recorder search.

## Deliverables
- Embedding abstraction API with pluggable providers
- Model selection matrix (quality/latency/cost/privacy tradeoffs)
- Normalization/versioning strategy for embedding vectors
- Operational policy for outages and fallback behavior

## Why this matters
Semantic quality depends as much on model governance as algorithm design. Unclear provider rules create unstable, expensive systems.

## Acceptance
- Provider interface supports swapping implementations without query-layer changes
- Selection rationale is documented and reproducible
- Fallback behavior is deterministic when embeddings unavailable","notes":"Claimed to evaluate and land semantic embedding provider governance contract for wa-prefixed bead parity.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GentleSnow","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:46.226779Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-12T03:53:30.615043-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-12T03:53:30.615043-05:00","close_reason":"Embedding provider governance/interface contract documented and validated in docs/flight-recorder/embedding-provider-governance-contract.md","labels":["architecture","epic","flight-recorder","track-semantic"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.5.1","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:46.226779Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.5.1","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:46.964617Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.5.1","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:47.006784Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"},{"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.5.1","depends_on_id":"wa-oegrb.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-12T06:06:47.058303Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}"}],"comments":[{"id":41,"issue_id":"wa-oegrb.5.1","author":"GentleSnow","text":"Validated existing governance contract at docs/flight-recorder/embedding-provider-governance-contract.md against bead deliverables: (1) pluggable provider abstraction API (EmbeddingProvider + Registry), (2) model quality/latency/cost/privacy selection matrix and governance policy, (3) normalization + immutable profile_id versioning contract, and (4) deterministic query/index fallback semantics when semantic path is unavailable. This satisfies acceptance requirements for wa-oegrb.5.1 and unblocks wa-oegrb.5.2/wa-oegrb.5.3.","created_at":"2026-02-12T08:53:23Z"}]}
@@ -1138,6 +1138,7 @@
 {"id":"wa-rsaf.2","title":"Formal session restoration verification via proptest","description":"## Goal
Use property-based testing (proptest) to formally verify session restoration correctness: for all valid mux states S, restore(snapshot(S)) is approximately equal to S (isomorphic up to PIDs and timestamps).

## Background and Motivation
Session persistence (wa-rsaf) is the most critical feature -- if restore produces incorrect state, users lose work. Traditional unit tests check specific cases but cannot cover the full state space. Property-based testing generates thousands of random valid states and verifies the isomorphism property holds for ALL of them.

## Technical Design

### Isomorphism Specification
Define approximately equal for mux states:
```rust
fn is_isomorphic(original: &MuxSnapshot, restored: &MuxSnapshot) -> bool {
    // Layout topology must be identical
    original.layout_tree.is_structurally_equal(&restored.layout_tree)
    // Pane count must match
    && original.panes.len() == restored.panes.len()
    // Scrollback content must match (up to trailing whitespace)
    && original.panes.iter().zip(restored.panes.iter())
        .all(|(a, b)| a.scrollback_trimmed() == b.scrollback_trimmed())
    // Cursor positions must match
    && original.panes.iter().zip(restored.panes.iter())
        .all(|(a, b)| a.cursor == b.cursor)
    // Working directories must match
    && original.panes.iter().zip(restored.panes.iter())
        .all(|(a, b)| a.cwd == b.cwd)
    // PIDs may differ (new processes)
    // Timestamps may differ (restored at different time)
}
```

### Property-Based Strategy
```rust
proptest! {
    #[test]
    fn snapshot_restore_roundtrip(state in arb_mux_state()) {
        let snapshot = capture_snapshot(&state)?;
        let restored = restore_snapshot(&snapshot)?;
        prop_assert!(is_isomorphic(&state, &restored));
    }
}
```

### Arbitrary State Generation
Generate valid mux states with configurable complexity:
- 1-20 windows, 1-10 tabs per window, 1-5 panes per tab
- Random split directions (horizontal/vertical) with valid ratios
- Scrollback: 0-10000 lines of random text with ANSI escapes
- Cursor positions within valid bounds
- Working directories from a pool of valid paths
- Agent types from valid set

### Edge Case Properties
```rust
// Empty state roundtrips
prop_assert!(is_isomorphic(&empty_state(), &restore(snapshot(empty_state()))));

// Single pane roundtrips
proptest!(|s in arb_single_pane_state()| ...);

// Maximum complexity roundtrips
proptest!(|s in arb_max_complexity_state()| ...);

// Mid-write snapshot (concurrent modification)
proptest!(|s in arb_state_with_pending_writes()| ...);

// Partial failure recovery
proptest!(|s in arb_state(), fail_point in arb_fail_point()| {
    // Inject failure during restore, verify partial state is consistent
});
```

### Implementation Location
- New: crates/wa-core/tests/proptest_snapshot.rs
- Depends on: wa-rsaf snapshot infrastructure

## Existing Code References
- wa-rsaf beads: MuxSnapshot schema, SnapshotEngine, layout restoration, scrollback injection
- proptest crate: strategy generation framework

## Dependencies
- Depends on wa-rsaf session persistence (needs snapshot/restore to be implemented first)
- Should be created alongside or immediately after wa-rsaf implementation

## Acceptance Criteria
- proptest strategies for arbitrary valid mux states
- Roundtrip property: restore(snapshot(S)) approximately equals S verified for 10000+ random states
- Edge cases: empty, single-pane, max-complexity, mid-write, partial-failure
- Isomorphism specification documented and implemented
- Regression: any snapshot/restore change must pass all proptest cases
- Run time: < 60 seconds for full proptest suite

## Specific Proptest Strategies

### Arbitrary Layout Trees
```rust
/// Generate arbitrary valid layout trees with controlled complexity.
fn arb_layout_tree() -> impl Strategy<Value = PaneNode> {
    let leaf = (arb_pane_id(), arb_cwd(), arb_pane_size())
        .prop_map(|(id, cwd, size)| PaneNode::Leaf {
            pane_id: id, cwd, size,
        });

    // Recursive: split nodes with 2 children, up to 6 levels deep
    leaf.prop_recursive(
        6,    // max depth
        50,   // max nodes
        2,    // items per collection (binary splits)
        |inner| {
            (arb_split_direction(), inner.clone(), inner, arb_ratio())
                .prop_map(|(dir, left, right, ratio)| PaneNode::Split {
                    direction: dir,
                    children: vec![left, right],
                    ratio,
                })
        },
    )
}

fn arb_pane_size() -> impl Strategy<Value = PaneSize> {
    (1u16..=500, 1u16..=200)
        .prop_map(|(cols, rows)| PaneSize { cols, rows })
}

fn arb_split_direction() -> impl Strategy<Value = SplitDirection> {
    prop_oneof![
        Just(SplitDirection::Horizontal),
        Just(SplitDirection::Vertical),
    ]
}

fn arb_ratio() -> impl Strategy<Value = f64> {
    (10u32..=90).prop_map(|r| r as f64 / 100.0)
}
```

### Random Scrollback Content
```rust
/// Generate random scrollback with realistic terminal output characteristics.
fn arb_scrollback_content() -> impl Strategy<Value = Vec<ScrollbackLine>> {
    prop::collection::vec(arb_scrollback_line(), 0..10000)
}

fn arb_scrollback_line() -> impl Strategy<Value = ScrollbackLine> {
    prop_oneof![
        // Plain text lines (most common)
        60 => "[a-zA-Z0-9 .,:;!?-]{0,200}"
            .prop_map(|text| ScrollbackLine::plain(text)),
        // Lines with ANSI color attributes
        20 => (arb_sgr_params(), ".{0,150}")
            .prop_map(|(sgr, text)| ScrollbackLine::attributed(text, sgr)),
        // Lines with wide characters (CJK)
        10 => prop::collection::vec(arb_cjk_char(), 0..80)
            .prop_map(|chars| ScrollbackLine::plain(
                chars.into_iter().collect::<String>()
            )),
        // Lines with hyperlinks (OSC 8)
        5 => (arb_url(), ".{1,50}")
            .prop_map(|(url, label)| ScrollbackLine::hyperlink(url, label)),
        // Empty lines
        5 => Just(ScrollbackLine::plain(String::new())),
    ]
}

fn arb_sgr_params() -> impl Strategy<Value = Vec<SgrParam>> {
    prop::collection::vec(
        prop_oneof![
            Just(SgrParam::Bold),
            Just(SgrParam::Italic),
            Just(SgrParam::Underline),
            (0u8..=255).prop_map(SgrParam::FgColor256),
            (0u8..=255).prop_map(SgrParam::BgColor256),
            (0u8..=255, 0u8..=255, 0u8..=255)
                .prop_map(|(r, g, b)| SgrParam::FgColorRgb(r, g, b)),
        ],
        0..5,
    )
}
```

### Random Process Configurations
```rust
/// Generate random process configurations for snapshot restoration testing.
fn arb_process_config() -> impl Strategy<Value = ProcessInfo> {
    prop_oneof![
        // Shell processes (most common)
        60 => (arb_shell_type(), arb_cwd(), arb_env_vars())
            .prop_map(|(shell, cwd, env)| ProcessInfo {
                command: vec![shell],
                cwd, env,
                process_type: ProcessType::Shell,
            }),
        // Agent processes
        20 => (arb_agent_type(), arb_cwd(), arb_env_vars())
            .prop_map(|(agent, cwd, env)| ProcessInfo {
                command: vec![agent.binary_name()],
                cwd, env,
                process_type: ProcessType::Agent(agent),
            }),
        // Interactive programs (vim, htop, etc.)
        10 => (arb_interactive_program(), arb_cwd())
            .prop_map(|(prog, cwd)| ProcessInfo {
                command: vec![prog],
                cwd,
                env: HashMap::new(),
                process_type: ProcessType::Interactive,
            }),
        // Unknown/custom processes
        10 => (arb_command_path(), arb_cwd(), arb_env_vars())
            .prop_map(|(cmd, cwd, env)| ProcessInfo {
                command: vec![cmd],
                cwd, env,
                process_type: ProcessType::Unknown,
            }),
    ]
}

fn arb_shell_type() -> impl Strategy<Value = String> {
    prop_oneof![
        Just("bash".to_string()),
        Just("zsh".to_string()),
        Just("fish".to_string()),
        Just("/bin/sh".to_string()),
    ]
}

fn arb_agent_type() -> impl Strategy<Value = AgentType> {
    prop_oneof![
        Just(AgentType::ClaudeCode),
        Just(AgentType::Codex),
        Just(AgentType::GeminiCli),
    ]
}

fn arb_cwd() -> impl Strategy<Value = PathBuf> {
    prop_oneof![
        Just(PathBuf::from("/home/user/project")),
        Just(PathBuf::from("/tmp")),
        Just(PathBuf::from("/home/user")),
        Just(PathBuf::from("/var/log")),
        Just(PathBuf::from("/home/user/projects/wezterm_automata")),
    ]
}

fn arb_env_vars() -> impl Strategy<Value = HashMap<String, String>> {
    prop::collection::hash_map("[A-Z_]{1,20}", ".{0,100}", 0..10)
}
```

## Criterion Benchmarks for Property Test Throughput
Add benchmarks in crates/wa-core/benches/proptest_throughput.rs:
- bench_arb_mux_state_generation: Measure generation throughput of arb_mux_state(), target >1000 states/s
- bench_isomorphism_check: Measure is_isomorphic() comparison for two 50-pane snapshots, target <1ms
- bench_roundtrip_single_iteration: Single capture-restore-verify cycle for a 20-pane state, target <50ms (enabling 10000 iterations in <60s budget)
- bench_arb_scrollback_generation: Measure generation throughput of 10000-line random scrollback, target >100/s

## Cross-References
- **wa-rsaf.1** (Unit tests): Unit tests in wa-rsaf.1 cover specific known-good and known-bad cases (e.g., restore a 3-tab layout with known split ratios). The proptest suite in this bead (wa-rsaf.2) complements unit tests by exploring the unknown state space -- finding edge cases that manual test design would miss. Failures discovered by proptest should be added as regression unit tests in wa-rsaf.1 using proptest proptest_regression attribute.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:24:28.433979Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-10T22:54:34.351628-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-10T22:54:34.351628-05:00","close_reason":"19 proptest tests across ~6600 random inputs verifying layout restoration, scrollback injection, process planning, and serde roundtrips","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-rsaf.2","depends_on_id":"wa-rsaf","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:24:28.433979Z","created_by":"jemanuel"},{"issue_id":"wa-rsaf.2","depends_on_id":"wa-29k1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:24:56.524049Z","created_by":"jemanuel"}]}
 {"id":"wa-ruam","title":"CLI: ruleset profile list/apply","description":"## What
Add CLI commands to list and apply ruleset profiles.

## Why
Operators need fast toggles without editing TOML manually.

## How
- `wa rules profile list`
- `wa rules profile apply <name>`
- Reload pattern engine after switch

## Success Criteria

 {"id":"wa-ruus","title":"Track 1: Basics - What is wa? Start watching. View status (5 min)","description":"# Track 1: Basics

## Purpose
Introduce wa to new users in under 5 minutes. They should understand what wa does, successfully start it, and see meaningful output.

## Target Audience
Users who just installed wa and want to understand what it does.

## Exercise Sequence

### Exercise 1.1: What is wa?
- **Type**: Information
- **Content**: Brief explanation with ASCII diagram
- **Duration**: 30 seconds
- **Goal**: User understands wa is a "terminal hypervisor for AI agents"

### Exercise 1.2: Check WezTerm
- **Type**: Verification
- **Requires**: WezTerm running
- **Action**: User runs `wa doctor` (or tutorial auto-checks)
- **Success**: "✓ WezTerm detected with 2 panes"
- **Fallback**: Simulation mode shows mock output

### Exercise 1.3: Start Watching
- **Type**: Interactive
- **Requires**: WezTerm running
- **Action**: User runs `wa watch` (tutorial may auto-start)
- **Success**: "wa is now watching 2 panes"
- **Duration**: 60 seconds

### Exercise 1.4: View Status
- **Type**: Interactive
- **Action**: User runs `wa status`
- **Success**: Tutorial explains each column
- **Duration**: 60 seconds

### Exercise 1.5: Your First Event
- **Type**: Simulated (unless agent present)
- **Action**: Tutorial injects mock compaction event
- **Success**: User runs `wa events` and sees it
- **Duration**: 90 seconds

### Track Completion
- Achievement unlocked: "First Watch"
- Prompt to continue to Track 2

## Testing
- Golden output tests for each exercise explanation
- Integration test for full track in sandbox mode
- E2E test with real WezTerm (optional)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 5 exercises implemented
- [ ] Each exercise has clear instructions and success criteria
- [ ] Sandbox mode works for all exercises
- [ ] Track completion triggers achievement
- [ ] Total time <5 minutes in testing","notes":"Implemented Basics track as 5 exercises in crates/wa-core/src/learn.rs (what-is-wa, doctor check, start watcher, view status, first event). Enabled simulation mode for all basics exercises. Updated achievement mapping so first_watch unlocks on basics.3 and first_event on basics.5/events.1. Added regression assertions for 5 exercises + all-simulatable basics. Validation: cargo test -p wa-core learn; cargo fmt --check; cargo check --all-targets; cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-18T17:46:13.680730807Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-02-11T00:47:48.204171-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-08T06:25:08.046374553Z"}

 {"id":"wa-s8j7","title":"Add wa robot workflow status command","description":"# wa robot workflow status

## Current State (CLI Stub Done)
✅ CLI subcommand structure added
✅ JSON schema created (wa-robot-workflow-status.json)
❌ Implementation returns not-implemented error

## What Remains
Query workflow_executions table for execution state.

## Implementation
1. Accept execution_id parameter
2. Query workflow_executions + workflow_step_log tables
3. Return status, current_step, step_logs, timestamps, error

## Testing Requirements
- Unit: Returns execution status by ID
- Unit: Shows step progress accurately
- Unit: Error code stability (E_EXECUTION_NOT_FOUND)
- E2E: See wa-n8cd for lifecycle scenario

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Queries workflow_executions table
- [ ] Returns real-time step progress
- [ ] Includes step_logs array
- [ ] JSON validates against schema
- [ ] Unit + E2E tests pass with detailed logging

 {"id":"wa-sgy2","title":"FTUI-09.3.a Compile-time guardrails against ratatui reintroduction","description":"## Background
Migration completion is fragile without guardrails that prevent ratatui/crossterm regressions from re-entering changed paths.

## Deliverables
- compile-time/static checks that fail when forbidden imports appear in migration-complete modules
- allowlist/exception mechanism with explicit expiry rationale
- developer guidance for resolving violations and selecting ftui-native alternatives

## Acceptance Criteria
- guardrails run in CI and locally with deterministic pass/fail output
- violation logs clearly identify file/module and remediation path
- policy prevents silent backsliding while allowing narrowly scoped, documented exceptions.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CalmLynx","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:14:44.745714205Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor","updated_at":"2026-02-09T05:34:40.948881601Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T05:34:40.9487521Z"}
